source.edition_note: Draft passage record derived from BS_C01_S02::BS_C01_S02_V01_VERSETEXT_01.

translation.literal_translation: Because there is instruction of what is universally established. (1)
translation.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.1: the passage denotes Brahman because it teaches what is already established everywhere in Vedanta as Brahman.
translation.interpretive_note: Sutra capsule for the preceding debate.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Sutra criterion for preserving Brahman-reference by pan-Vedantic continuity.
Normative textual indication used as inferential ground for referent determination.
Conclusion that the debated sentence belongs to Brahman-doctrine rather than finite-self discourse.

uncertainty_notes: Long bhashya blocks translated as draft prose and require line-level philological curation.
Terminology retained in mixed transliteration/English where doctrinal precision is required.
